sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the output, I see:
- Bar region: sky blue at col 39, blue at col 41
- Left panel: purple cells at (51, 15), (54, 12), (54, 18), (57, 15) - this is a cross pattern without the center!
Wait, the left panel has purple cells at:
- (51, 15): top
- (54, 12): left
- (54, 18): right
- (57, 15): bottom
This is a hollow cross pattern (4 cells), not a filled cross pattern (5 cells)! The center cell (54, 15) is not purple, it's part of the gray background.
So the left panel has a hollow cross pattern, not a filled cross pattern. This matches the grid's white cross cells (top, left, right, bottom).
But the grid has the center cell as light green (N), which is not part of the hollow cross pattern. The left panel has the center cell as gray (part of the background).
So to make the grid match the left panel, I need to:
1. Make the center cell gray (not light green).
2. Keep the cross cells as white (or any non-gray color).
3. Keep the corner cells as gray.
The center cell is currently light green. I need to make it gray. Clicking on the center cell cycles it between gray and light green. So I need to click on it once to change it from light green to gray.
Let me try this.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# The left panel has a hollow cross pattern (4 cells), not a filled cross pattern (5 cells).
# The grid has white cross cells (top, left, right, bottom) and a light green center cell.
# To match the left panel, I need to make the center cell gray.
# Let me click on the center cell to change it from light green to gray.
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 55, 'col': 30}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
